english: Central Asia. The tea called _bru-táṅ ('bru tang) is considered the best, and of other teas Cs. mentions rtsé-ja (rtse ja), zì-liṅ-spú-ja (zi ling spu ja) hairy (?) tea from Siling, (a province in the neighbourhood of the Kokonor); Schr.: ɣnám-ja (gnam ja), míṅ-ja (ming ja), _bó-ja ('bo ja), ja-ɣzúṅs (ja gzungs), zàu (zau), hu-čág (hu cag), _u-sì ('u si); bzaṅ-ja (bzang ja), or ko-tse (ko tse) is, acc. to Cs., good ordinary tea, čuṅ- _jug (chung 'jug), or čuṅ-čuṅ (chung chung) are sorts of inferior quality. The shepherds in W. make use of a surrogate, viz. the Potentilla Inglisii (spáṅ-ja) (spang ja), growing on the mountains at a height of 15 000 feet; poor people in Sik. use the leaves of the maple (ɣya-lí) (gya li). Other comp. ja-bkrúg (ja bkrug) (pronounced *jhab-ṭúg*), prob. for ja-dkrúg (ja dkrug), twirling-stick Ts. — ja-mčód (ja mchod), libation of tea. — ja- _tág (ja 'tag), or btág (btag) grinding-stone, in India and Tibet used for kitchen purposes inst. of our little mortars. — ja-dám (ja dam) Sch. tea-pot (?) — ja-blúg (ja blug) W. a little pitcher-shaped brass vessel. — ja- _bíṅ (ja 'bing) (pronounced *jham-biṅ*) C. tea-kettle, tea-pot. — já-ma (ja ma) the man that prepares the tea in a monastery, tea-cook; jai dpon (ja'i dpon) head-tea-cook. — ja-rìl (ja ril) 1. W. grinding-stone; 2. Lex. skull. — ja-sun-ċan (ja sun can) 'a cup of tea, or: as much as a cup of tea' Sch. — ja-seg (ja seg) tea-dust Sch.

robbing, robbery, jag rgyág-pa (jag rgyag pa) to rob, to be a robber; rku-jag-gyu-zól byéd-pa (rku jag gyu zol byed pa) Glr.; jág-pa (jag pa) frq. robber (not robbery Sch.); jag-dpón (jag dpon) captain of a gang of robbers Mil.

1. num. fig.: 37. — 2. the correlative form of the pron. čì (ci), what. For the construction of a sentence containing čì (ci) or jì (ji), v. gaṅ (gang) II. The explanation there given shows, that in correct language jì (ji) is always followed by a participle: jì yód-pa de púl žig (ji yod pa de pul zhig) offer what you have, make a libation of what you have. Owing, however, to the slight difference in the pronunciation of čì (ci) and jì (ji), the former is frq. written in the place of the latter; jì (ji), of course, is used in conjunction with the same words as čì (ci); a few more instances may follow here: jì-skad (ji skad) whatever, relative to words spoken: ṅas jì-skad smras kyaṅ (ngas ji skad smras kyang) whatever I may say Glr. — jì-snyéd (ji snyed) 1. as much as, as great as; 2. C. very much, every thing possible. — jì-lta-ba (ji lta ba) 1. adj. of what kind, of what nature, ... jì-lta-ba bžín-du ... la yaṅ de-bžín-no (ji lta ba bzhin du ... la yang de bzhin no) as it is with ... so it is with ... Stg. 2. sbst. quality, nature, condition Cs. — jì-lta-bu (ji lta bu) such as, like as, Lat. qualis. — jì-ltar (ji ltar) adv. as, in what manner; 'a-mas jì-ltar zér-pa bžín-du ('a mas ji ltar zer pa bzhin du) according to what the mother has said Glr. — jì-ste = čì-ste (ji ste = ci ste). — jì nús-kyis (ji nus kyis) to the utmost, to the best of one's ability Dzl. — jì ma jì-bžín-du (ji ma ji bzhin du) (?) according to custom or common usage Sth. — jì-mi-snyám-pai bzód-pa (ji mi snyam pa'i bzod pa) a patience prepared for every event Sch. (?) — jì-tsam = jì-snyéd (ji tsam = ji snyed); lo lṅa lón-pa jì-tsam-pa de-bžín-no (lo lnga lon pa ji tsam pa de bzhin no) they are (as tall) as (children) five years old Stg.; jì tsam byas kyaṅ (ji tsam byas kyang) whatever they had done Tar.; jì-tsam-na (ji tsam na) or nas (nas) as soon as, when. — jì-bžín (ji bzhin) as, like, how, jì-bžín འtso mi ruṅ (ji bzhin 'tso mi rung) (he) can in no wise, by no means, continue to live Lt.; jì-bžín-du ɣsuṅs (ji bzhin du gsungs) elliptically: he said how (it was), he answered according to the state of the case (Schf.) Tar. 89, 9. — jì-sríd (ji srid) as long as.
denotes a way of drawing lots by threads of different colours, whence a class of Bonpos is called ṕya-bon ju-tíg-čan (phya bon ju thig can) Glr.
Liš., *ju-lúm* (ju lum) W., a globular stone used for grinding spices, = ja-rìl (ja ril).
a sort of silk stuff Cs.

1. Sch.: 'possessed of good manners, of propriety of conduct, decent, agreeable; jus-bdé (jus bde) sincere' (?) — 2. Cs. clever, skilled, able, experienced. *žiṅ-gi lẹ* (zhing gi le) in agriculture, *mag* (mag) in military matters C.
1. num. fig.: 97. — 2. a particle, used for expressing the comparative de-
